summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering adoption of the 2026 Land Use Plan, approval of equipment purchases for the service department, personnel reassignments in the police department, and new appointments to the Board of Zoning Appeals and Zoning Commission. Community forum discussion included veteran banner/flag displays and ways to honor military veterans, and a presentation was given by Drew from Clermont County Community and Economic Development on the township's land use plan.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed unanimously
Motion to approve payment of bills (March 24 – April 14, 2026) – passed unanimously
Resolution R-013-2026 adopting the 2026 Goshen Township Land Use Plan – passed unanimously
Motion to approve purchase of John Deere 5100E cab tractor from Co-ing Equipment not to exceed $74,132.20 – passed (Allen yes, Hauserman yes, Hodges abstain)
Motion to approve purchase of Tiger 60-inch side mount rotary mower combo from Dexter Company not to exceed $38,620 – passed (Allen yes, Hauserman yes, Hodges abstain)
Motion to approve sale of surplus/obsolete items on GovDeals – passed unanimously
Motion to approve indigent burial of Dora M. Bailey not to exceed $911 – passed unanimously
Motion to approve $1,000 sergeant-level sponsorship for Clermont County Law Enforcement Appreciation Banquet – passed unanimously
Motion to approve appropriation in Station 18 construction fund 4901-220-360 in the amount of $1,043,585.55 – passed unanimously
Motion to increase appropriations in zoning contracted services fund by $3,500 (from $5,000 to $8,500) – passed unanimously
Motion to approve Memorial Day parade on Monday, May 25th at 10 AM – passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn to executive session at 6:46 PM – passed unanimously
Motion to reassign Detective Lunsford from investigations to road patrol effective May 4, 2026 – passed
Motion to reassign Officer Moen from road patrol to investigations as detective effective May 4, 2026 – passed (Allen absent)
Motion to reassign Officer Lines from road patrol to investigations as detective effective May 4, 2026 – passed (Allen absent)
Motion to approve updated job description reclassifying part-time administrative assistant Jen Farmer as part-time finance and wellness coordinator with no salary change – passed (Allen absent)
Motion to appoint Clarence A. Groves Jr. as alternate member of Board of Zoning Appeals (term through December 31, 2028) – passed (Allen absent)
Motion to appoint Tammy Purcell from alternate to regular member of Zoning Commission (term through December 31, 2026) – passed (Allen absent)
Motion to adjourn at 7:29 PM – passed (Allen absent)
action_items: Drew to send email with notes on land use plan changes and forward-looking items to the board
Administrator/staff to explore options for a military/veterans memorial display area at the township
Donna to proceed with Memorial Day parade planning for May 25th
Drew/Clermont County Economic Development to engage Goshen Township in county-wide economic development strategic plan and land use coordination
Chief to coordinate move into new Station 18 the week of April 27th
Medic unit at Ford for repair – expected 4-6 weeks for return
Staff to list surplus items on GovDeals (filing cabinet, mailbox setup, manufactured home, 1973 Oliver 1365 tractor, 1999 John Deere 4510 with sidebar)
key_amounts: $38,620 – Tiger 60-inch side mount rotary mower combo purchase
$911 – indigent burial of Dora M. Bailey
$1,000 – sponsorship for Clermont County Law Enforcement Appreciation Banquet
$1,043,585.55 – appropriation in Station 18 construction fund to finish paying bills
$3,500 – increase to zoning contracted services fund (from $5,000 to $8,500 total)
$250 – donation acknowledged from/to Goshen Township Historical Society for Goshen Derby event
$120,961 – total donations by Citizen Police Academy Alumni Association from 2010 to 2026, sponsoring 290 children for Shop with a Cop
notable_topics: 2026 Goshen Township Land Use Plan presentation and adoption (Resolution R-013-2026)
Community discussion on veteran banner/pennant flags on utility poles and Duke Energy restrictions
Alternative ideas for honoring military veterans with memorial displays
Purchase of new John Deere tractor and mower attachment replacing 1973 and 1999 equipment
Station 18 construction nearing completion – move-in expected week of April 27th
Clermont County economic development strategic plan and USDA farmland preservation grant
Reclassification of Jen Farmer to finance and wellness coordinator
Appointments to Board of Zoning Appeals and Zoning Commission
Sale of surplus items including manufactured home (former fire station temporary quarters)
Memorial Day parade approval for May 25th
Clermont County Land Bank involvement and Comb site cleanup
Tri-State Peer Support Team recognition letter for Jen Farmer
attendees: Drew (Clermont County Community and Economic Development)
Steve Payton (public speaker, Zoning Commission member)
Jen Farmer (part-time administrative assistant/finance and wellness coordinator)
Clarence A. Groves Jr. (BZA alternate appointee)
notes: Trustee Hodges abstained from votes on John Deere tractor and mower purchases due to conflict of interest (works in John Deere equipment sales). Trustee Allen left the dais at 7:25 PM due to a personal emergency and was absent for several votes after executive session. The 2026 Land Use Plan supersedes the 2000 growth management plan and 2008 State Route 28 corridor plan. Drew announced an Ohio Department of Agriculture grant for county-wide land use coordination expected to be completed by mid-2027. The last land use plan was done 26 years ago (circa 2000). Duke Energy previously denied permission for veteran banner flags on their utility poles. The manufactured home being surplused was used as temporary fire department quarters during Station 18 construction.
title: BOT 4-14-26
